description_long: This is a vintage-style lantern with a weathered, rust-colored metallic body and a prominent cylindrical glass enclosure at its center. The lantern features two sturdy, curved side handles that connect the base to the top, providing both structural support and a means for carrying or hanging. A wire loop arches above the lantern, likely serving as a hanging handle. The base is octagonal and slightly wider, with a round knob or cap on one side, possibly for adjusting or refilling the fuel. The glass enclosure is protected by a thin metal wire cage, which wraps around it in a crisscross pattern. The overall design suggests durability and functionality, suitable for outdoor or urban environments.
description: A weathered, rust-colored metal lantern with a cylindrical glass enclosure, side handles, and a wire hanging loop. The lantern features a protective wire cage around the glass and a round adjustment knob on the base.
description_view_0: Front view showing the full glass enclosure, both side handles, the wire hanging loop, and the adjustment knob on the base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the lantern, the thickness of the handles, and the protruding adjustment knob on the right.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front but without the adjustment knob, showing the back of the glass enclosure and handles.
description_view_3: Opposite side view with the wire hanging loop and no visible adjustment knob, emphasizing the lantern's symmetry.
